channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa and their dog were dead for some time, warrant shows
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and one of their dogs were apparently dead for some time before a maintenance worker discovered their bodies at the couple's Santa Fe home, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, FELICIA FONSECA and BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FDA cancels meeting to choose flu vaccine strain
item.description: The cancellation of the FDA meeting, alongside a similar postponement last week of a meeting of Centers for Disease Control and Prevention vaccine advisors, is adding to the growing concern among scientists and public health advocates that Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oscar-winning actor Gene Hackman, wife found dead and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Utah is one step away from becoming 1st state to ban fluoride in water
item.description: The Utah governor's signature is all that stands between a state ban on water fluoridation after House Bill 81 cleared its final hurdle in the legislature on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court hears arguments on straight woman's 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NFLPA report cards: Dolphins, Vikings finish at the top for second straight year
item.description: The Miami Dolphins are the top-ranked team, followed by the Minnesota Vikings, for the second consecutive season in the NFL Players Association report card.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Recent aviation disasters and close calls stoke fears about the safety of flying
item.description: All the recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington D.C. last month was the worst disaster.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Michelle Trachtenberg, 'Gossip Girl' and 'Buffy' actress, dead at 39
item.description: Actress Michelle Trachtenberg has died, police sources confirmed to multiple news outlets on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote in an op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Judge dismisses racketeering indictment against New Jersey Democratic power broker, co-defendants
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — A state judge on Wednesday agreed to toss out racketeering charges against New Jersey Democratic power broker George Norcross and those charged alongside him.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Southwest Airlines flight abruptly rises to avoid another plane crossing Chicago runway
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Pilots on a Southwest Airlines flight attempting to land at Chicago's Midway Airport were forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and KATHLEEN FOODY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Korea's Yoon defends his martial law decree as impeachment ruling nears
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a final statement at his impeachment trial, South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol defended his martial law decree that plunged the country into chaos as a bid to inform the public of the danger of the opposition-controlled parliament as he vowed Tuesday to push for politi
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Things to know about the ruling blocking President Trump's refugee ban
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's effort to suspend the system for resettling refugees in the U.S. is on hold after a federal judge in Seattle blocked it. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge indefinitely blocks Trump’s plan to freeze federal aid
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday indefinitely blocked the Trump administration’s freeze of federal funding, dealing a stark blow to President Trump’s sweeping efforts to realign government spending with his agenda. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about the Feb. 28 Economic Blackout
item.description: Customers across the United States are looking to send a message Friday to companies that have rolled back their DEI initiatives in the form of a 24-hour consumer boycott, also known as the “Feb. 28 Economic Blackout.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Man caught hiding $10K of cocaine in toupée
item.description: A man was arrested in Columbia after trying to board a flight while wearing a wig hiding cocaine worth more than $10,000.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Terror designation might prompt cartels to target Americans, expert says
item.description: The designation empowers the Treasury Department and U.S. law enforcement to aggressively target financial resources these groups utilize to produce their product, wage war on each other and authorities, and terrorize civilians in their home countries.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Live updates: House Republicans adopts budget after Trump whips GOP holdouts
item.description: House Republican leadership is working to pass a budget resolution on Tuesday to pave the way for President Trump's agenda, but opposition has emerged from all sides of the conference.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Funeral set for an officer killed while responding to a gunman in a Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A funeral service will be held later this week for a Pennsylvania police officer shot and killed while responding to a hospital intensive care unit where authorities said a gunman had shot three hospital employees and taken staff members hostage last weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Coast Guard rescues 3 boaters stuck at sea for 9 hours after their boat capsizes
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Coast Guard crews rescued three boaters in Captiva Island, Florida, after their boat flipped over and they were stuck at sea for nine hours. A family member reported concerns at 1 a.m. Sunday after not hearing from the three boaters since 8 p.m. Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge declines to restore AP access to White House amid 'Gulf of America' dispute
item.description: A federal judge on Monday declined to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces open to the media at the White House, including the Oval Office amid the Trump administration’s battle against the wire service over its refusal to use “Gulf of America” in its popular stylebook. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, ABC 4

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4
